findings.title: method-effectiveness.js uses snapshot date directly without next-day entry shift
findings.description: method-effectiveness derives forward returns from `asOf = fname.replace('.json','')` (the methods-history file name, which is `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` written by snapshot-methods-history.js). It then computes entry price at `nearestTradingDay(asOf, map)` — the SAME calendar day the snapshot was taken. snapshot-methods-history.js runs in the workflow before US market close (afternoon UTC), so the same-day price already contains the intra-day move triggered by data the script just observed. walk-forward-perf.js explicitly fixes this via F-BT-001 getEntryDate(asOfRaw) → next day if asOf < 21:00 UTC. method-effectiveness.js never applies the same shift. This is silent look-ahead-bias in every per-method alpha calculation. Backtest results are inflated by approximately the average intraday US-market drift after the snapshot timestamp.
findings.evidence: scripts/method-effectiveness.js line 141: `const asOf = fname.replace('.json','');` — same-day entry, no 21:00 UTC gate. Compare scripts/walk-forward-perf.js line 114-124 (getEntryDate) and line 258 (`const entryDate = getEntryDate(asOfRaw);`).
findings.fix: Read each methods-history file, extract a timestamp (currently the file only stores `date: today`, NOT the asOf timestamp from when the snapshot was written), then apply getEntryDate equivalent. Better: have snapshot-methods-history.js record `asOfTimestamp` alongside `date`, and have method-effectiveness apply the same next-day shift as walk-forward-perf.

findings.title: backtest-10-weeks.js and backtest-from-history.js use TODAY's method evaluation against past prices
findings.description: Both scripts call `Runner.evaluateStock(stockData)` on the CURRENT snapshot to derive pass/fail counts, then attribute the cohort's 10-week historical return to those flags. Both files self-document this as 'Forward-Looking-Bias. Methoden-Werte sind HEUTE.' This is acknowledged but the scripts still write `backtest-10w-result.json` containing 'alphaAvg' that is genuinely non-causal: the pass set is conditioned on TODAY's fundamentals, which themselves are the result of the very price moves being measured. A turnaround stock that 10-weeked +200% will trivially be in pass-cohort today because revenue growth swung positive. The output file is treated as a backtest result downstream (no flag in the JSON marks it as 'demonstration only'). Risk: anyone reading backtest-10w-result.json without reading the script believes a 30%+ alpha.
findings.evidence: backtest-10-weeks.js line 12 header acknowledges, but line 144 writes 'alphaAvg' to backtest-10w-result.json with no flag like `forward_looking_bias: true`. backtest-from-history.js line 92 writes same key.
findings.fix: Either delete these scripts (superseded by walk-forward-perf.js + method-effectiveness.js which use historical methods-history) OR mark output JSON with `methodology: 'forward-looking-bias-demo'` and refuse downstream consumption.

findings.title: method-effectiveness.js universe = today's snapshot list, never frozen to vintage time
findings.description: Unlike walk-forward-perf.js (which now uses `picksFile.evaluatedTickers` after F-BT-003), method-effectiveness iterates `file.stocks` from methods-history/YYYY-MM-DD.json. That set is whatever stocks existed in ./snapshots/ when the methods-history script ran — i.e. the entire historical universe AT VINTAGE TIME, which is fine on the surface. BUT delisted/removed tickers between vintage time and TODAY are silently dropped at the price-lookup step: `if (!map) continue;` (line 177). The pass-cohort and fail-cohort therefore both lose delisted names equally, but delisted is usually correlated with failure → pass-cohort survives intact, fail-cohort loses its worst exits, so alpha is biased UP. The note string (line 298) says 'Yahoo universe survivor-biased' but no mechanism corrects it.
findings.evidence: scripts/method-effectiveness.js line 174-177: silent skip on missing price index entry. No accounting of how many fail-cohort members were dropped due to missing prices vs pass-cohort members.
findings.fix: Log and emit `passDroppedNoPrices` / `failDroppedNoPrices` per (method, horizon) so the user can see the asymmetric attrition. Treat methods where fail-drop-rate >> pass-drop-rate as having inflated alpha.

findings.title: Gaps in picks-history vintages silently shrink alpha-vs-universe sample without warning
findings.description: picks-history/ contains 2026-05-08, 09, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15 — the 5-12 file is missing (weekend or workflow failure). walk-forward-perf.js iterates all available files but the 28d window from 2026-05-08 ends on 2026-06-05 and the 84d window from any 2026-05-x vintage requires future data — so when the script runs, some 84d windows are pending. Vintage-count is reported correctly, but `picks-regression-check.js` does `priors.slice(-8)` 'up to last 8 weeks' (line 118, comment says 'weeks' but slice is 8 FILES not 8 weeks) — when files are daily-not-weekly (now), 8 'priors' = 8 days which crosses a 7-day band, but 'MIN_HISTORY_RUNS = 4' threshold is then evaluated against count of files, not against time-spanning runs. If the workflow shifts cadence, drift detection silently miscalibrates.
findings.evidence: scripts/picks-regression-check.js line 116-118 comment says 'up to last 8 weeks of history' but `.slice(-8)` on daily files = 8 days. Bug #32 fixed vintageCount vs totalPicks alignment; this is a parallel issue.
findings.fix: In picks-regression-check.js: filter priors by date-distance (e.g. last 56 days) rather than count, so cadence changes don't break the heuristic. In walk-forward-perf.js: emit `vintageGapDays` per vintage so consumers see when 84-day windows cross holes.

findings.title: Benchmark fetched once at entry date but evaluatedTickers universe and per-pick prices use different nearestTradingDay snaps
findings.description: computeBenchmarkReturn (line 209-227), computeUniverseMedianReturn (line 159-178), and the per-pick loop (line 286-306) each call nearestTradingDay independently. For the SAME asOf date, SPY's price map might have a trading day on D-1 while a Hong Kong ticker has D+0 — entryDate inconsistency means SPY return is D-1→D+27 while pick return is D+0→D+28, a 1-day skew that compounds with regional time-zones (HK/TW close 8h before NYSE). For 7-day horizons the skew can be 14-28% of the window. Combined with 100+ picks per mode and median-comparison, this introduces noise but more importantly an asymmetric one: regional picks are systematically benchmarked against a US-market window that ends sooner.
findings.evidence: scripts/walk-forward-perf.js: line 170 entryDate uses ticker's own map, line 221 entryDate uses SPY's map, line 300 each pick uses its own map. No anchoring to a single 'pipeline date'.
findings.fix: Compute a single anchor pair (entryDate, exitDate) per (vintage, horizon) from SPY's map alone. Then for each pick / each evaluated ticker, look up p0 at `anchor.entry` (skip if missing in that ticker) and p1 at `anchor.exit`. Trading-day misalignment then becomes a coverage statistic rather than a silent skew.

findings.title: Price returns blend USD, TWD, HKD, EUR without normalization — alpha is unit-soup
findings.description: pull-historical-prices.js stores `{ date, close: latestClose }` where `latestClose` is the Yahoo adjclose in the LOCAL currency of the ticker's exchange. 2026-05-15 picks include 2345.TW (TWD), 6699.HK (HKD), and ALAB (USD). returnPct (line 100-103) computes percentage so currency cancels for the per-ticker return — BUT only if the currency was the same on entry and exit dates. If TWD strengthens vs USD by 2% in the 28-day window, a flat 2345.TW position shows +2% local return but 0% USD return. The median of returns across this mix is meaningless: the implicit currency is the unweighted median of the picks' base currencies. SPY return is pure USD. Therefore alphaVsBenchmark = (mixed-currency median) − (USD median). The note `currency: result.meta.currency` is stored only in the day-file snapshot, not in history.json, so walk-forward can't even detect this.
findings.evidence: pull-historical-prices.js line 78 stores currency in `todaysSnapshot[stock.ticker]` (per-day file) but line 84 only adds `{ date, close }` to history.json — currency dropped. walk-forward returnPct line 100-103 has no FX normalization.
findings.fix: Either (a) pull FX series and normalize all prices to USD before storing in history.json, or (b) restrict the universe-median/benchmark comparison to a single currency block (US-listed only), or (c) emit per-pick currency tag and let downstream filter.

findings.title: MIN_SAMPLES=10 threshold applied only to per-horizon alpha; vintage-aggregate medianAlpha has no minimum-vintage floor
findings.description: Per-vintage alpha is suppressed when n < 10 (line 316: `n >= MIN_SAMPLES`). But the cross-vintage `medianAlphaVsBenchmark` (line 444) is computed by `median(collect('alphaVsSpy'))` where collect filters nulls. If only 2 vintages have ≥10 evaluated picks at 84d, medianAlphaVsBenchmark = median of 2 values — reported with no warning that the cross-vintage n is 2. method-effectiveness.js has MIN_VINTAGES=4 gate; walk-forward summary has no such gate. Downstream methodology-report.md displays α vs SPY as a single number with no '(n=2 vintages)' qualifier — invites over-confidence.
findings.evidence: scripts/walk-forward-perf.js line 438-451: data.summary[key] builds medianAlpha* with no insufficient-evidence flag based on vintageCount. methodology-report.js line 60 prints α directly.
findings.fix: Add `medianAlphaVsBenchmarkEvidence: 'ok' | 'insufficient-vintages'` to data.summary[key], gated on `vintageCount >= 4` (mirror method-effectiveness MIN_VINTAGES). methodology-report.md should render 'n.a. (vintages=2)' instead of suggestive point values.

findings.title: Walk-forward replays stored pass flags but doesn't capture method-version drift
findings.description: Picks-history vintages store `mustPassCount`, `score` and the ranked top-100 of each mode AT VINTAGE TIME using whatever Runner/strategy-modes.js logic existed that day. walk-forward-perf.js uses those stored picks (correct) but `backtest_runner_version: 'stored_pass'` (line 356) is a placeholder string — there is no actual method-version hash anywhere. If today the QUALITY_COMPOUNDER mustPass set is changed, historical pick lists are still treated as comparable to today's live picks for alpha aggregation. The F-BT-011 note in walk-forward-perf line 463 acknowledges this but the JSON has no method-set digest to detect the drift programmatically.
findings.evidence: walk-forward-perf.js line 356 hardcodes 'stored_pass'. Older picks-history files (2026-05-08, 09, 10, 11) lack `score` field — proves method-set has drifted; snapshot-picks.js line 96 added the null-safe fallback (F-BT-009) as evidence of past breakage.
findings.fix: Have snapshot-picks.js write `methodSet: { hash: sha256(sortedMethodIds + version), version: PIPELINE_VERSION }` into each picks-history file. Walk-forward groups vintages by methodSet.hash; cross-version aggregates emit a warning.

findings.title: Daily price retention strategy can break adjClose consistency across stock splits
findings.description: pull-historical-prices.js line 75 reads `adjclose` from Yahoo. Each daily run REPLACES Yahoo's full 400-day adjclose curve only for new entries via `history[stock.ticker].push({date, close: latestClose})` — but it doesn't retro-adjust the older entries when a split occurs between runs. Suppose AAPL has 7:1 split on 2026-05-20: Yahoo's adjclose for 2026-05-10 retroactively divides by 7. The local history.json still has the pre-split adjclose for 2026-05-10 (recorded on 2026-05-10), and the post-split adjclose for 2026-05-20 (recorded that day). returnPct then computes (post-split / pre-split) = artificial −85% return for any 28-day window crossing the split. Tag 148 commit added adjclose READ, not historical replay.
findings.evidence: pull-historical-prices.js line 80-87: only TODAY's adjclose is added to history; pre-existing entries are never re-adjusted. Line 86 `slice(-400)` retains old (unadjusted-to-today's-corporate-actions) values.
findings.fix: On every run, replace ALL history entries with Yahoo's freshly-returned adjclose curve (not just append today's). Yahoo's adjclose is retroactively split-adjusted; trusting it requires re-pulling and re-writing the full curve, not appending.

findings.title: Cross-mode duplicates inflate cross-mode alpha aggregations (same ticker counted N times)
findings.description: snapshot-picks.js dedupePicksByCompany (line 105-125) is called WITHIN each mode loop. A ticker like NVDA can simultaneously land in HYPERGROWTH and QUALITY_COMPOUNDER; both modes record it with its score. walk-forward then computes per-mode medianReturn independently — fine per mode. But the SAME NVDA return contributes to two different per-mode alphas, and the methodology-report.md aggregates all three modes side-by-side as if independent. A bull rally that lifts dual-tagged mega-caps gets credited 2× across the modes table without any cross-mode adjustment. Compounds with universeMed which uses evaluatedTickers (all 4147 unique) — denominator is unique tickers, numerator (per-mode pickMed) treats overlaps independently.
findings.evidence: snapshot-picks.js line 251 deduplicates within `picks` array (single mode's pickStockForMode results); no cross-mode dedupe step. methodology-report.js line 53-60 prints all modes' alphas in one table.
findings.fix: Emit `crossModeOverlap` matrix into picks-history each day: { (HG, QC): 12 tickers, ... }. methodology-report should warn when reading modes whose overlap > 20% of the smaller mode's picks: 'these alphas share inputs'.

findings.title: addDaysIso uses UTC calendar arithmetic that drifts past DST regional close times
findings.description: addDaysIso(asOf, 7) returns asOf's calendar date +7 UTC days. For a US-pre-market snapshot at 13:04 UTC (asOf=2026-05-15T13:04Z), getEntryDate shifts to 2026-05-16, then +7d → 2026-05-23. Saturday. nearestTradingDay snaps to Friday 2026-05-22 (within ±5 days). The intended 7-trading-day window has become 6 trading days (Fri to Fri = 5 close-to-close). Inconsistent across vintages: vintages launched on Tuesdays get 7-day windows ending on next Tuesday (7 trading days), vintages launched on Fridays get 6 trading days. Combined with the small MIN_SAMPLES, this skews the 1w horizon median estimate by up to 17%.
findings.evidence: scripts/walk-forward-perf.js line 105-109 uses pure calendar days. line 287 `addDaysIso(entryDate, days)` with days ∈ {7,28,84}, no trading-day arithmetic.
findings.fix: Replace addDaysIso with a trading-day step (count K business days forward from anchor, skip US holidays). Or document that 'days' means calendar days and rename HORIZONS_DAYS to HORIZONS_CALENDAR_DAYS, then state the median-trading-days delivered.

findings.title: snapshot-picks.js evaluatedTickers list is the FULL snapshot universe — not just stocks Runner could evaluate
findings.description: F-BT-003 fix intended to make universe-median use only tickers actually evaluated at vintage time. But snapshot-picks.js line 218-220 builds evaluatedTickers from `stocks.filter(s => s && s.meta && s.meta.ticker).map(s => s.meta.ticker)` — every snapshot file present, regardless of whether Runner.evaluateStock succeeded. The `_pipelineFailures` set (line 198-207) collects stocks where evaluateStock threw, but those tickers are STILL in evaluatedTickers (the filter only checks meta.ticker, not whether evaluations.get(ticker) is defined). When pipeline-health failure rate hits 4%, walk-forward computes universe-median over 4% non-evaluable tickers (returning NaN at the method level, but the price-level return can still be computed). This dilutes universeMed downward relative to the true evaluatable-universe and inflates alphaVsUniverse.
findings.evidence: snapshot-picks.js line 218-220: filter only checks meta.ticker presence. Lines 197-207 build `evaluations` Map; should be: `evaluatedTickers = Array.from(evaluations.keys())`.
findings.fix: Replace line 218-220 with `const evaluatedTickers = Array.from(evaluations.keys());` — only tickers Runner could evaluate are reference-universe.

findings.title: method-effectiveness.js cache replay path contains dead/broken code that obscures logic
findings.description: Lines 148-165 implement a cache-hit replay path that uses `cached.find(x => x.ticker === ticker && x.methodId === methodId && x.ret === ret)?.pass` — but at that point we're iterating `for (const { ticker, methodId, ret, quality } of cached)`, so the `pass` is already destructurable from the loop variable. The find() lookup is broken (it tries to match on values from the surrounding destructure scope that doesn't exist there cleanly) AND lines 218-233 then DELETE the perMethod state and rebuild it ALL over from cache anyway. Result: the cache-hit fast path on line 148 contributes 0 useful work — it builds buckets that get discarded. Performance regression at minimum; if the rebuild loop ever has a bug, the script still runs but produces silently wrong output.
findings.evidence: scripts/method-effectiveness.js line 213-218: 'NOTE: The cache replay loop above has a bug for the pass lookup — re-do it cleanly by rebuilding perMethod entirely from scratch'. The author noted the bug and chose to layer a workaround rather than fix the cache path.
findings.fix: Delete lines 148-165 entirely (the if-cachedDates-has branch is dead). The rebuild loop at 218-233 already covers all cases. Or fix the cache-replay path and remove the rebuild.

findings.title: backtest-pass-vs-fail.js dateAddDays uses local-timezone Date arithmetic
findings.description: dateAddDays uses `new Date(dateStr)` (interprets dateStr in local time on parse) then `setDate(getDate()+days)` then `toISOString().slice(0,10)`. In timezones west of UTC, the ISO string can shift by one day on DST boundaries (March/Nov in US). prices/history.json dates are pure UTC. Off-by-one matches cause `series.find(e => e.date === date)` to miss exact-match and fall through to `series.find(e => e.date >= date)` — the find returns the FIRST entry whose date >= asOf, which may be many days later if there's a gap. Silent skew at the cohort-aggregation level.
findings.evidence: backtest-pass-vs-fail.js line 23-27: no 'T00:00:00Z' anchoring, no UTC accessor. Compare scripts/walk-forward-perf.js line 105-109 (UTC-anchored).
findings.fix: Replace with the addDaysIso pattern from walk-forward-perf.js.

findings.title: picks-regression-check.js MIN_HISTORY_RUNS=4 but priors.slice(-8) — silent threshold confusion
findings.description: MIN_HISTORY_RUNS=4 gates the drift detection (line 145-148: returns 0 without alerting if priors.length < 4). The slicing uses last 8 priors (line 118). If the workflow runs daily, priors are 8 daily files (= ~10 calendar days). The median of 8 same-week daily counts is essentially noise — daily counts don't move 35% day-over-day under any reasonable normal-market scenario, so the drift detector is hard to trigger. Meanwhile a slow week-over-week creep gets buried in the daily-noise median. The comment 'last 8 weeks of history' (line 118) is wrong: it's last 8 FILES, which is currently 8 days.
findings.evidence: picks-regression-check.js line 118 comment vs reality. Picks-history directory currently has 7 daily files, none weekly.
findings.fix: Filter priors by a calendar-day threshold (e.g. files where date < today AND date >= today − 56 days). Also rename MIN_HISTORY_RUNS to MIN_HISTORY_DAYS and check the date-span of available priors.

findings.title: computeBenchmarkReturn benchmark choice is fall-through, not regime-matched
findings.description: Candidates are tried in fixed order ['SPY', 'QQQ', 'IWM'] — if SPY is present (always since pull-historical-prices.js force-pulls it), QQQ/IWM are never used. But the universe spans US large-cap (HYPERGROWTH heavy on semiconductors → QQQ would be more apt), small-cap turnarounds (TURNAROUND → IWM is the closer benchmark), and international (no proxy at all). A single SPY benchmark for all three modes mixes apples & oranges in the methodology-report. Tag 134 acknowledged this conceptually with three benchmarks listed but the runtime always picks SPY.
findings.evidence: scripts/walk-forward-perf.js line 212-214: first-truthy break. methodology-report.js line 55 has one column 'α vs SPY' regardless of mode.
findings.fix: Map mode → benchmark explicitly: HYPERGROWTH → QQQ, QUALITY_COMPOUNDER → SPY, TURNAROUND → IWM. Or accept the limitation explicitly in the report and add a `benchmarkMatched: boolean` flag.

findings.title: compute-picks-lookback.js evaluates today's picks against 1Y/3Y/5Y past returns — pure look-ahead
findings.description: Script header (line 13-18) is explicit about look-ahead bias and survivorship bias, but the output file (`lookback-YYYY-MM-DD.json`) carries no machine-readable flag. processMode line 70-97 computes `outperf{N}y` against SPY and writes to JSON. If anything downstream reads this file (e.g. dashboard), it consumes a metric whose semantics are 'how have today's picks would have performed if you had bought them N years ago' — which is purely retrospective curation. No flag distinguishes this from forward returns.
findings.evidence: compute-picks-lookback.js line 118 writes `out` without methodology marker. Output filename pattern overlaps with walk-forward outputs in feeling.
findings.fix: Add `methodology: 'look-back-only-survivor-biased'` field to output JSON. Or rename file to `survivor-look-back-YYYY-MM-DD.json` so semantic confusion is reduced.

findings.title: method-effectiveness bootstrap CI hardcodes 200 resamples; insufficient for small groups
findings.description: BOOTSTRAP_RESAMPLES=200. For 95% percentile CI, you draw at index 5 (= floor(200*0.025)) and 195. With only 200 samples, the 2.5th-percentile estimate has wide simulation noise: a fair-coin bootstrap-CI at 200 reps is ±~3pp from the true CI bound for typical alpha magnitudes. Methods where alpha is borderline-significant (e.g. CI lo95 = +0.1pp) can flip to non-significant on re-runs purely due to bootstrap noise. Methodology report uses CI lo95 > 0 as the binary 'significant' flag.
findings.evidence: scripts/method-effectiveness.js line 41 (`const BOOTSTRAP_RESAMPLES = 200;`), line 95-96 percentile index `Math.floor(alphaSamples.length * 0.025)`.
findings.fix: Increase to 2000 for production runs (10× cost, still fast). Or add a 'borderline_significant' bucket for methods where |CI_lo / CI_width| < 0.1.

top_priority: F-BT-006 (currency-mixed returns medianed against USD benchmark)
F-BT-005 (per-ticker independent nearestTradingDay creates regional skew)
summary.notes: Tag 32 fixed vintageCount vs totalPicks alignment in walk-forward summary; F-BT-003 added evaluatedTickers; both are visible and respected in walk-forward-perf.js. The OPEN audit-level concerns are: (1) method-effectiveness.js does not mirror walk-forward's getEntryDate logic (F-BT-001), (2) currency handling is missing across the board (F-BT-006), (3) the older backtest scripts (backtest-10-weeks/from-history) still write authoritative-looking output despite known forward-looking bias (F-BT-002), (4) cache-replay code in method-effectiveness has a documented broken branch shadowed by a workaround (F-BT-013).
